title: Over 100 Missing, 78 Injured After Suspected Submarine Sinks Iranian Ship | 10 News
description: At least 78 people have been injured and over 100 are still missing after a suspected submarine attack on an Iranian warship.  Around 30 sailors have been rescued off the coast of Sri Lanka after the Iris Dena issued a distress call.  It’s not clear who may have carried out the attack. title: 'They Are Toast': US, Trump Claims 'Strong Position' In Iran | 10 News
description: US President Donald Trump says US forces are in a "very strong position" in Iran and are making "tremendous progress" just four days into the conflict with Iran.  "They (the Iranian regime) are toast, and they know it. Or at least, soon enough, they will know it," said US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth during a briefing in Virginia on Wednesday.  The President is still "actively considering" US's role, if any, in Iran post-conflict, according to White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt. title: Three Hurt After Plane Crashes Into Homes In Arizona | 10 News
description: Three people have been injured after a small plane struck two homes in Arizona before landing nose-down next to a swimming pool in a backyard.  The Piper P-28 was carrying a student pilot and flight instructor headed to Deer Valley Airport in Phoenix.  Local authorities said the instructor, student and a man who was in one of the homes at the time of the crash, were taken to hospital. title: Marine Veteran Injured At Capitol Hill Protesting US Conflict With Iran | 10 News
description: Veteran Brian McGinnis, who served in the US Marine Corps from 2000 to 2004  was physically ejected from a Capitol Hill hearing on Wednesday for protesting against US and Israel's conflict with Iran.  Footage has emerged online of the encounter, which captures several police officers and Montana's Republican Senator Tim Sheehy trying to pull McGinnis out of the room. During the scuffle, the marine's hand is caught in the door and you hear a crack as people in the gallery scream that his 'arm is broken'.  While his injuries were not confirmed, it was confirmed McGinnis was placed under arrest and transported to the hospital.  McGinnis is a Green Party candidate, running for election to the US Senate for North Carolina. US Capitol Police say he now faces several charges, including assaulting a police officer and resisting arrest.  Green Party official Mark Elbourno told CBS News that they deny their candidate assaulted anybody. title: US Hails Sinking Of Iranian Ship Near Sri Lanka As Kurdish Fighters Begin Ground Invasion | 10 News
description: Another flight full of Australians escaping the conflict in the Middle East is heading home.  But it comes as the US takes a dramatic step in its war with Iran.  An American submarine has sunk an Iranian ship off the coast of Sri Lanka - a first since World War 2.   Dozens are dead and even more are injured as the US Secretary of War declares Iran is 'toast'.
